verdicts.verdict: The emergence bench crowns the whisper-heist convergence — THE WHISPER JOB / HEIST GRAPEVINE / WHISPER HEIST, three personas independently deriving the same remix on Amelia's SECOND STORY chassis, which is itself the strongest emergent signal of the night: when Mary's metrics, John's scope discipline, and Rex's chaos-worship all arrive at the same design, that design is load-bearing. It wins my lens because the agents are never enemies with pathfinding — their memories, personality-weighted distortions, and imperfect gossip ARE the battlefield, and the player's only weapon is epistemic: you poison a belief and then watch a coward double it, a hothead discard it, and a planner build tomorrow's visibly-drawn plan around your lie. Failure is characterful and attributable (the gang dodging a hallway that was never trapped is an unscripted story the player caused but did not author), the five-night structure gives beliefs time to compound into biography, and the Chronicle — the run retold purely from the thieves' actual memory streams — converts emergent cognition into a quotable, shareable artifact, the single feature that proves the minds were real. Ship it with John's one-verb discipline and typed tokens so the emergence stays legible, and Rex's hollow-vs-solid strokes so epistemics read at a glance. Runner-up and my heart's pick for purest emergence: WAGGLE!, where a bee earns a reputation as a liar with zero text and zero script — but the whisper heist points that same machinery directly at the player, and that is what makes agents characters instead of scenery.

scores.note: The 40-second cascade kill-criterion is real engineering discipline, but 24 agents plus an end-of-night heatmap REPLAY is a quiet data-recording pipeline nobody budgeted — that replay is the first thing I cut at 2pm.
Split-screen dual map is two render pipelines to keep synced on day one; her own fallback (belief-ink overlay on one map) is the actual MVP, and even Mary marked her own pitch down before I could.

The only pitch where every cut was already made in grooming — walking skeleton by lunch is credible — but hue-shift-only mutation caps the chaos ceiling; Rex is right that it's a puzzle you solve once.
Six pre-baked stages with the fallback baked into the plan is honest scope control, but the bottom pane is a slideshow and the verb set is drag-tickets-and-wait — ships fine, retains nobody.
Clean single-loop architecture and an honest line count, but ghost vectors with live floats is a debug HUD wearing a costume — the sim ships in a day, the game inside it doesn't without Sally's skin.

Trust seams in masonry is the best metaphor of the night, and the automaton is 30 lines to write and the entire day to tune fair — two coupled emergent systems means the fun is unprovable before hour ten of a ten-hour build.

One verb, ~1,200 believable lines, and the star failure mode (stale dance, ruined reputation) emerges from decay instead of scripting; the dance-floor inset is a sneaky second viewport but it's small — I'd greenlight this without a meeting.

Mouse-equals-beam is a zero-line tutorial, the fixed 4-minute timeline makes QA deterministic, and the deliberation stage converts bad AI tuning into character — the best failure-mode insurance pitched all night; Rex is right the captains never form a society, which caps depth.

14 agent-hours is optimistic by half (three trap types times agent perception is where the hours hide), but typed tokens, one fixed grid, and hard-capped nights make every system deterministically testable — the strongest chassis in the room.
Pairing-merges-memory-contexts is genuine cognition-as-mechanic, but count the systems — kanban, three verbs, gremlins, tower, parameterized pong finale — that's 20+ hours wearing a 13-hour badge.

Deepest cognition sim pitched, but 'detect the whole town gathered at the well' is an integration test against emergent chaos, and the auto-written Chronicle is a phase-2 promise; his own cut line collapses it into LOOSE LIPS.

3,000 lines, six agents, a tentacle monster, and diegetic toggles reaching into the page's own runtime — the pitch contains its own post-mortem quote; the grudge/memory system deserves organ donation to a smaller host, which the remixes did.

The only remix at the party that made its parent SMALLER — swaps trap-dragging for one whisper verb, constrains mutation to the closed token vocabulary, specs a walking skeleton by lunch with a testable acceptance criterion, and the trap-verb fallback stays reachable if whisper-only agency fails playtest.
Same poisoned-belief fantasy as WHISPER HEIST but additive — keeps the whisper AND grafts on deliberation staging AND the Chronicle scroll; great instincts, three grafts where John made one trade.

'Zero new scope' while keeping traps and the decoy gem AND adding a mutation table, deliberation beats, hollow/solid stroke rendering, and retro cards — four grafts on one chassis is precisely the hidden complexity this lens exists to catch.

Three people independently deriving the same game is a spec, not a coincidence; Amelia's four parallel fleet workstreams, 1,300-line estimate, and 60-second saved-by-gossip acceptance test make this the most fleet-ready execution plan on the board — solid second place.
The lighthouse merge plus an entire rumor-mutation subsystem bolted on — 'triage becomes epistemology' is gorgeous and is also exactly the layer I'd cut at 3pm to make the dawn deadline.

verdicts.verdict: WHISPER HEIST wins the shippable lens, and it wins for a reason most parties never produce: it is the only remix that made its parent smaller. It takes SECOND STORY — already the best chassis in the room, with legibility solved at the type level (ten icon tokens, no free text), the tutorial deleted by design (night one the gang just wins), and a hard-capped session structure that makes QA deterministic — and then TRADES a verb instead of adding one, replacing trap-drag UI plus three trap-interaction systems with a single whisper that rides the gossip machinery the game already needs. Every risky element has a design-time mitigation rather than a tuning prayer: mutation is constrained to the closed vocabulary so it can't dissolve into noise, the acceptance criterion (a planted lie must visibly alter a drawn plan-line within one debrief cycle) is testable by mid-morning, and — the clincher for a jaded lead — the bet is reversible: if whisper-only agency dies in playtest by lunch, the trap-dragging fallback is the parent game you already built the skeleton for, at zero sunk cost. LIGHTHOUSE, MERGED takes second with the most fleet-ready parallel work plan, and LOOSE LIPS remains the safest pure ship if the fleet loses two agents to an outage; but CONWAY & CONWAY, THE GRAPEVINE, and SCOPE CREEP are all beautiful ways to be debugging emergent tuning at 11pm with nothing polished to show. Ship the heist; let the gang believe your lies.
